summary: Organized the monolithic `include/functions.h` and `include/variables.h` declarations into Doxygen modules as a temporary aid for eventually removing those catch-all headers. The PR also changed `Doxyfile` so Doxygen scans `include` in addition to `src` and `docs`, making the new module annotations visible on the generated Modules page.

key_files.why: Expanded Doxygen INPUT from `src docs` to `src include docs` so header-level module annotations are parsed.
Large-scale regrouping of function prototypes under `/// @addtogroup ...`, `/// @{`, and `/// @}` blocks.
Grouped globals into Doxygen modules, converted inline uncertainty notes into Doxygen comments, and replaced `#pragma once` with an `_variables_h_` include guard.

what_changed: Added Doxygen module grouping around many prototypes in `include/functions.h`, including examples such as `lbcollision lbCollision`, `lbarchive lbArchive`, `cmcamera cmCamera`, `ftfighter ftFighter`, `ftanim ftAnim`, `ftparts ftParts`, `ftcommon ftCommon`, `ftcaptain ftCaptain`, `ftmasterhand_* ftMasterHand_*`, `grground grGround`, `item itUnk1`, `hsd_*`, and runtime/MSL groups.

Converted several old inline comments into structured Doxygen notes, for example `/// @todo vararg may start earlier`, `/// @todo static`, `/// @todo UnclePunch Map file: AS_203_PassiveWalljump_Walljump`, and a documented return note for `func_800804EC` using `/// @returns <tt>fp->x40*fp->x34</tt>`.

Moved some declarations into more specific groups instead of leaving them in broad address-order blocks, such as splitting fighter declarations into `ftAnim`, `ftParts`, `ftAction`, `ftCamera`, `ftColl`, `ftCommon`, and other fighter submodules.

In `include/variables.h`, added module groups for `ftgigakoopa ftGigaKoopa`, `ftfighter ftFighter`, and `db_unknown_001 dbUnk1`; preserved the note about `lbl_804D64FC..lbl_804D6550` being initialized in `Fighter_LoadCommonData` as a Doxygen `@todo`.
Changed `Doxyfile` INPUT to include the `include` directory, enabling the new header annotations to be rendered by Doxygen.
The PR body explicitly described this as a temporary measure to make removing `functions.h` and `variables.h` easier.
smart_moves: Used Doxygen modules rather than mechanically splitting files immediately, creating a lower-risk intermediate map of which declarations belong to which eventual header/module.
Preserved uncertain reverse-engineering knowledge as searchable Doxygen `@todo` comments instead of dropping old inline comments during cleanup.
Kept unknown or unresolved areas explicit with names like `lbUnk1`, `ftUnk5`, `dbUnk1`, `todo_unk NOT FOUND`, and numbered Master Hand groups, avoiding false certainty.
Made Doxygen scan `include`, which is necessary for header-only declarations to appear in module documentation.
Used group IDs that correspond to likely source/module names while exposing readable titles, e.g. `ftfox_specialn ftFox_SpecialN` and `dolphin_mtx PSMTX`.
decomp_lessons: For monolithic catch-all headers, a Doxygen grouping pass can document ownership before physically moving declarations into per-module headers.
When old comments contain uncertain matching or naming knowledge, convert them into `@todo`, `@returns`, or other structured Doxygen comments so future decomp work can search and triage them.
Do not over-name uncertain code just to make documentation pretty; numbered unknown groups are preferable to invented semantics.
Doxygen changes may need build/config updates, not just header annotations; verify that `Doxyfile` INPUT includes the directories where annotations live.
Module grouping can serve as a migration plan for deleting `functions.h` and `variables.h` by showing which declarations need replacement includes.
naming_conventions: Doxygen group syntax used: `/// @addtogroup <group_id> <DisplayName>`, followed by `/// @{` and closed with `/// @}`.
Group IDs generally use lowercase subsystem/file-like names such as `lbcollision`, `lbarchive`, `cmcamera`, `ftanim`, `ftcommon`, `grground`, and `hsd_cobj`.
Display names generally preserve project subsystem capitalization such as `lbCollision`, `lbArchive`, `cmCamera`, `ftAnim`, `ftCommon`, `grGround`, `HSD_CObj`, and `PSMTX`.
Unknown groups are explicitly numbered or marked unknown, e.g. `lbunknown_001 lbUnk1`, `pl_unknown_001 plUnk1`, `ft_unknown_006 ftUnk6`, `db_unknown_001 dbUnk1`, and `todo_unk NOT FOUND`.
Fighter special/action modules use names like `ftFox_SpecialN`, `ftNess_AttackS4`, `ftNess_SpecialHi`, and `ftMasterHand_02`.
Uncertain symbolic notes were expressed with Doxygen markup such as `@todo @c DataOffset_PlayerScale_MultiplyBySomething` and references like `#Fighter_LoadCommonData`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was evidenced; this PR was primarily a documentation/header organization change.

The only matching-adjacent evidence is preservation of uncertainty around declarations, such as vararg placement for `func_80016C64`, possible static status for some functions, and map-file hints like `AS_203_PassiveWalljump_Walljump`.

review_feedback.body: Needs a conflict resolution but LGTM

review_feedback.note: No line-level review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that actually remove or split `include/functions.h` and `include/variables.h` after this Doxygen grouping pass.
Search for Doxygen groups introduced here, such as `ftcommon`, `ftparts`, `lbarchive`, and `hsd_cobj`, to see which became real headers/modules.
Search for later resolution of `@todo vararg may start earlier` on `func_80016C64`.
Search for later cleanup of `todo_unk NOT FOUND` declarations around `func_8037ABC8`, `func_8037AD20`, and `func_8037CF98`.
Search for later naming of `lbl_804D64FC..lbl_804D6550` and confirmation of their relation to `Fighter_LoadCommonData`.
